content.text: Consonant clusters are different in Greek from English consonant clusters. For example, p and n are not pronounced together in English. However, in Greek, pi plus nu are regarded as one consonant cluster.
Note that consonant clusters with gamma have the following pronunciations:

rows: Not found in English, like ing in sing followed by the ch of loch.

content.text: Since Greek and English consonant clusters are different, one way to check if a combination of clusters is a Greek consonant cluster is to look it up in a dictionary.  Any combination of consonants that begins a Greek word is a true consonant cluster elsewhere as well.
